summary: This lecture from the Muslim Community Center of Tucson focuses on the Islamic concept of Tawhid (monotheism) and Shirk (associating partners with Allah). The speaker discusses Allah's first command and prohibition in the Quran: to worship only Allah and not ascribe similars unto Him. The lecture explains that Shirk is the unforgivable sin and can manifest in many subtle forms, emphasizing that ignorance is not a valid excuse in the modern age of readily available knowledge. The speaker also discusses how to properly establish prayer (Salah) and the role of the Masjid as a place of worship that should bring out the best in people, contrasting it with the marketplace.
suggested_title: Tawhid and Shirk: The First Command and Prohibition of Allah
